transcript.clean_text: stage six of the tour de swiss was due to take the riders up into the mountains in the end it did however with 29 riders less than initially planned covert 19 causing the withdrawal of three complete teams bahrain uae aral alpacine having to go home also the race leader alexander vlasov unable to take on this wonderful route taking up two huge climbs both or category finishing on maus alp after 177 kilometers the race did get underway 12 riders got up the road faster maznada the best placed in the general classification but all the way down at nine minutes and four seconds he wasn't a danger and israel premier tech had no intention of chasing him down with jacob fulson the new leader quinn simmons took the maximum points over the gigantic newfound pass that meant that he would almost secure the king of the mountains jersey with two and a half days still to go israel premier tech were just controlling the gap trying to make sure that nobody could threaten fulsang's lead but garrett thomas sat in second place at a single second when he came to the intermediate sprints the first was won by michael matthews up there in the breakaway with andrea pasqualon both sprinters battling it out matthews would take the second sprint and move into the points classification lead and once we got onto the final climb almost 18 kilometers long it was a quick sort out of the strongest legs mazda the amanda really pushed the pace from the bottom behind ineos grenadiers were using up the few resources they had tom pidcock one of the 29 who couldn't start due to covert 19. gwen simmons was dropped with 11.2 kilometers to go but he keep working at it behind maznada and denze out the front in the end they're before up the top as shampoo radha came back shakman attacking out the peliton but not really going very far jakob fusang chasing him right down that meant that danny martinez would do a good job and at the head of the race the fall together would look and look at each other maznara the first to attack again with three kilometers to go but once more he couldn't get rid of anybody and with 400 meters to go quinn simmons was back and almost looked like he was going to take the stage ahead inside the final hundred meters then shampoo came around the lookout for dents in the black the big ruler at the top of a all category mountain and winning it on the photo finish a 28 year old from germany taking his first ever world tour success i think he looked a little pleased about it behind garrett thomas tried to force a split a little bit of sprint on the way in but he gita closed the gap and there would be no change in the top two at least with thomas still trailing jakob pulsang by a single second dens then beating champusa errada simmons and maznada pasher and talmum with combative rides then the big bunch a lot of calories expended by nicodems a big opportunity with that cake to take them back but who's going to take the cake in the gc jakub full sang still one second ahead of count right thomas sergio gita up to third at 10 it's very very tight at the top but the dane who was second last year is first at the moment with two stages to go there'll be plenty of anxiety overnight to see what might happen with a covered situation again but if all's well they'll take on some big big passes again they'll be heading up into liechtenstein this time the luck mania pass is the longest climb of this particular tour de swiss but another all-category finish in malbun after 194.6 kilometers still all to play for it's still very close we hope for drama on the bike this time less off it let's see how it goes heading into stage seven of the tour de swiss you can watch it on gcn plus and discovery plus you
